title: Computer Systems: A Programmer's Perspective
subtitle: CS:APP
author: Randal E. Bryant
themes: Writing efficient and correct C programs through system knowledge
The memory hierarchy and its impact on performance
Process management and virtual memory abstraction
Dynamic memory allocation and garbage collection
Concurrent programming with threads and synchronization
awards: Used as the primary text for CMU's legendary 15-213 course
Widely adopted at top CS programs worldwide
ACM SIGCSE Best Paper Award for associated lab design
Standard reference for systems interview preparation
excerpt: This book is about the computer system from the perspective of a programmer — the interface between software and hardware. Understanding how a computer actually executes code is essential for writing efficient, correct, and secure programs.
